goal: Implement a real devnet async conditional SPL escrow buy where the user deposits USDC_TEST into program-controlled escrow, a Solana program validates the oracle price condition on-chain, and execution pays out SOL from a prefunded vault PDA to the user while routing settled USDC_TEST to treasury.

context.summary: Current code has a simulated conditional_buy_sol quote flow and an AgentActionGuard oracle gate that only marks approvals executed. There is no real escrow, no SOL vault payout, and the current frontend approve flow expects an unsigned transaction for Phantom injected. This feature requires real on-chain settlement in devnet, with backend/keeper only monitoring and triggering while the Solana program remains the source of truth.

acceptanceCriteria: A user can create a devnet order signed with Phantom injected that deposits USDC_TEST into an escrow SPL token account controlled by a PDA.
The program owns order state, escrow authority, SOL vault authority, and validates execution conditions on-chain.

execute_order succeeds only when the configured oracle feed matches, price is less than or equal to target_price_usd_e8, oracle data is fresh enough, confidence is within threshold, escrowed USDC_TEST is sufficient, and the SOL vault has enough lamports.
Successful execution atomically transfers required USDC_TEST from escrow to TREASURY_USDC_ATA and desired SOL lamports from SOL_VAULT_PDA to the user recipient.
If max_usdc_in would be exceeded, escrow is insufficient, oracle checks fail, or the SOL vault lacks liquidity, execution fails safely without partial settlement.
The user can cancel or reclaim funds in the allowed lifecycle states without backend custody.
Frontend UX uses Phantom injected only, exposes order/escrow/oracle/vault states clearly, and does not present the flow as a Jupiter or DEX swap.
Backend keeper and API can monitor and trigger execution, but cannot bypass program checks or act as source of truth.
constraints: Do not write implementation artifacts outside the intended production areas when execution starts; this planning handoff only writes docs/devnet-conditional-escrow-buy-sol/.
Do not introduce Jupiter integration or describe this flow as a market DEX swap.
Use env-config placeholders for unknown devnet addresses such as USDC_TEST_MINT, TREASURY_USDC_ATA, SOL_VAULT_PDA, and PYTH_SOL_USD_FEED.
Backend/keeper may sign only their own trigger transactions and must not hold user signing authority.
All task statuses must remain pending with empty evidence in this initial plan.
decisions: Prefer a dedicated Anchor program for conditional escrow buy settlement over further expanding AgentActionGuard, because custody and settlement are now first-class responsibilities.
Model the order as a real on-chain account with explicit lifecycle, escrow balances, oracle config, and execution results.
Use a PDA-controlled SPL token escrow for USDC_TEST and a prefunded PDA-controlled SOL vault for lamport payout.
Use the associated token account for (ESCROW_AUTHORITY_PDA, USDC_TEST_MINT) as the escrow token account unless implementation constraints require a custom token account.
Compute required USDC on-chain from desired_sol_lamports and oracle_price_usd_e8 using checked u128 math and enforce max_usdc_in.
Return any leftover USDC_TEST to the user during execute_order when possible to avoid stranded escrow dust.
Keep backend responsibilities to proposal preparation, order indexing, monitoring, status APIs, and optional execution triggering.
Frontend remains self-custodial: Phantom injected signs create/deposit and user-driven cancel/refund transactions.

tasks.title: Scaffold the dedicated Anchor program for conditional escrow settlement
tasks.description: Create the new program workspace or equivalent extension point, define the crate structure, and establish the base account/instruction modules for vault config, order state, escrow authority, and settlement lifecycle.
verification: Anchor workspace builds with the new program present.
Program declares placeholder instruction set and account types without conflicting with existing agent-action-guard workspace.

tasks.title: Implement vault config and PDA layout
tasks.description: Define PDA seeds, config accounts, escrow token authority, escrow token account derivation, and SOL vault derivation. Include admin bootstrap and pause/config safety checks for devnet.
verification: Seeds and account sizes are deterministic and documented in code.
Vault config stores placeholders for treasury, mint, USDC decimals, and oracle feed.
Program exposes initialize_vault_config and fund_sol_vault or equivalent bootstrap flow.
SOL vault is derived and validated by seeds rather than trusted from backend-provided config.

tasks.title: Implement order creation and escrow deposit
tasks.description: Add create_order_and_deposit or equivalent instruction so the user can create the order and move USDC_TEST from their ATA into PDA escrow in a self-custodial Phantom-signed flow.
verification: Instruction validates desired_sol_lamports, max_usdc_in, target_price_usd_e8, recipient, expiry, and mint constraints.
Escrow token transfer uses SPL checked transfer semantics.
Escrow token account is validated as the ATA for ESCROW_AUTHORITY_PDA and USDC_TEST_MINT.
Order cannot exist in an unfunded open state after the happy-path create flow.

tasks.title: Implement oracle parsing and checked settlement math
tasks.description: Add on-chain validation for the expected oracle feed, price freshness, confidence threshold, and required USDC calculation using checked u128 math and e8 normalization.
verification: Program rejects stale oracle data, high confidence intervals, invalid price signs, and wrong feed accounts.
required_usdc calculation is deterministic, uses ceil rounding, and is covered by unit tests.
Math enforces max_usdc_in and prevents overflow.

tasks.title: Implement execute_order atomic settlement
tasks.description: Implement the execution path that validates lifecycle state, computes required USDC, transfers escrowed USDC_TEST to treasury, pays SOL from the prefunded vault PDA to the recipient, and records execution results atomically.
verification: Execution fails safely when the SOL vault lacks liquidity or escrow is insufficient.
Execution validates treasury ATA, escrow ATA, mint, and PDA seeds before moving funds.
Execution succeeds only when price is less than or equal to target and max_usdc_in is respected.
No partial transfers remain on failure.

tasks.title: Implement cancel, expire, refund, and reclaim lifecycle paths
tasks.description: Add the user-authorized cancellation and post-expiry reclaim logic so open orders can safely return escrowed USDC_TEST and close or mark order state consistently.
verification: User can cancel an open order before execution under the defined policy.
Expired orders cannot execute and can return funds safely.
State transitions prevent double refund or double execution.

tasks.title: Add program unit and integration tests
tasks.description: Cover vault bootstrap, order creation/deposit, oracle gating, successful execution, insufficient escrow, insufficient SOL vault liquidity, cancellation, expiry, and reclaim paths.
verification: Tests prove atomic settlement and safe failure behavior.
Tests prove oracle and math checks block invalid execution.
Tests prove lifecycle paths cannot be replayed.

tasks.title: Create devnet bootstrap scripts and env contract
tasks.description: Add scripts or operational helpers to configure devnet placeholders, initialize vault config, derive PDAs, fund the SOL vault, and document required environment variables for local/demo operation.
verification: Bootstrap steps document how to configure USDC_TEST_MINT, TREASURY_USDC_ATA, SOL_VAULT_PDA, and PYTH_SOL_USD_FEED.
Devnet operator can fund and inspect the SOL vault before frontend testing.

tasks.title: Update backend proposal and approve contracts for real escrow orders
tasks.description: Replace the simulated conditional_buy_sol contract with a real escrow-order proposal shape and an approve response that returns a Phantom-ready unsigned transaction for create_order_and_deposit.
verification: Proposal payload includes desired SOL, target price, max USDC, recipient, expiry, and execution mode.
Approve no longer pretends to execute the buy and instead returns an unsigned transaction for Phantom injected.
No backend contract claims Jupiter or market swap integration.

tasks.title: Implement backend order indexer, status APIs, and keeper trigger
tasks.description: Add backend services that index on-chain orders, expose list/detail APIs for frontend consumption, and optionally trigger execute_order when observed conditions appear favorable, while deferring final truth to the program. The MVP indexer polls program accounts every 15 seconds, filters per user by Order.user, refreshes observed oracle executability every 10 seconds, triggers execute_order as a permissionless caller with backoff after repeated failures, and exposes derived observed_executable separately from confirmed on-chain status.
verification: Backend can list and fetch order status by connected wallet without mutating truth off-chain.
GET /api/conditional-orders?user=<pubkey> returns only orders whose on-chain Order.user matches the requested wallet.
Polling cadences are documented/configurable and distinguish observed_executable from confirmed on-chain status.
Keeper trigger path signs only its own execution transaction, has no special on-chain privileges, and records failures clearly.
API responses distinguish observed executability from confirmed on-chain state.

tasks.title: Implement Phantom create/deposit flow in frontend
tasks.description: Wire the proposal approval UX so ConditionalBuyProposalCard and related state logic request the unsigned create/deposit transaction, pass it to Phantom injected, and surface order identifiers and escrow status after confirmation.
verification: Frontend handles awaiting_signature, submitted, confirmed, cancelled, and failed states for order creation.
Wallet errors and transaction errors are mapped distinctly.
The UI references Phantom injected and not Phantom Embedded.

tasks.title: Build order detail and monitoring UI
tasks.description: Add quiet data-product surfaces for order summary, escrow balances, oracle condition, SOL vault status, and execution timeline in desktop and mobile layouts.
verification: UI shows order, escrow, oracle, vault, and lifecycle state clearly without marketing styling.
Users can understand that this is a controlled devnet escrow settlement rather than a Jupiter or DEX swap.

tasks.title: Add backend and frontend tests for the real order flow
tasks.description: Cover schema changes, approve behavior, Phantom unsigned transaction flow, order list/detail parsing, and UX state transitions for creation, cancellation, and observed execution outcomes.
verification: Tests fail if the flow regresses to simulated execution semantics.
Tests prove approve returns unsigned create/deposit transactions for Phantom.
Tests prove status rendering and error mapping for the new lifecycle.

tasks.title: Run end-to-end devnet validation and finalize operational docs
tasks.description: Validate the full demo path in devnet: bootstrap config, fund SOL vault, create order with Phantom, observe keeper/manual execution, and verify settlement balances and UI states end to end.
verification: An end-to-end devnet run confirms real escrow deposit and real SOL payout.
Observed balances and transaction signatures align with order status in the UI.
Open issues and operational caveats are documented.

risks: Oracle normalization, staleness, and confidence handling may be implemented incorrectly and allow bad execution decisions.
A mixed design that keeps adding settlement logic into AgentActionGuard can create authorization and upgrade complexity.
The prefunded SOL vault introduces liquidity and operational risk in devnet demos if not monitored and replenished.
USDC_TEST mint/decimals or treasury configuration may differ from assumptions and break settlement math or transfers.
Partial UX assumptions from the current simulated flow may leak into the real flow unless proposal and approve semantics are fully replaced.
validation: Anchor build passes for the chosen program layout.
Program tests cover creation, execution, cancellation, expiry, reclaim, and safe failure conditions.
Backend/API tests confirm real-order contracts and keeper non-authoritative behavior.
Frontend tests confirm Phantom injected unsigned-transaction flow and lifecycle rendering.
Devnet end-to-end run confirms USDC_TEST escrow deposit, SOL payout, treasury routing, and correct UI status transitions.
